do_not_copy: Do not copy source visuals, layouts, screenshots, brand marks, case-study prose, or presentation assets.
observed_move: Open with a high-confidence product promise before technical detail, giving the audience a single reason to care before the mechanism appears.
why_it_works: A commercial AI keynote has to lower buyer anxiety quickly. The opening earns attention by framing the system as a strategic capability, then lets proof arrive later instead of crowding the first impression.
ppt_translation: Use an editable low-density PPT cover with one focal object, one decisive headline, and no more than two support labels. Build the focal object from native shapes or a simple SVG so the slide remains PowerPoint-editable.
quality_risk: The cover can become generic if the promise is abstract or if the focal object has no concrete relationship to the product decision.

extraction_units.derived_rule: Open with one concrete commercial promise before any mechanism or workflow proof appears.
extraction_units.execution_guard: Do not place multiple product claims or proof cards on the cover.
extraction_units.qa_probe: The contact sheet communicates the product promise before small text is readable.

extraction_units.source_anchor: commercial AI keynote need for premium first impression
extraction_units.derived_rule: Build the cover's focal object from editable native shapes or a simple SVG tied to the product decision.
extraction_units.execution_guard: Do not use copied visuals or rasterized headline text as the core claim.
extraction_units.qa_probe: The focal object remains editable and has a stated relationship to the deck decision.
